channel.title: Workday Scraper - RSS Feed
channel.description: An RSS feed for new Workday postings.

item.title: Intel: Supply Chain SnOP Program Manager
item.description:
locations
US, California, San Jose
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
time left to apply
End Date: October 31, 2024 (6 days left to apply)
job requisition id
JR0267985
Job Details:
Job Description:
In Q4 2023, Intel announced Altera will be reported as a separate business unit beginning on January 1, 2024, with ongoing support from Intel. This position is associated with that standalone business strategy and is expected to fully transition to a standalone company at some time in the future.
The Supply Chain SnOP Program Manager is accountable for the oversight of the Sales and Operations Planning (SnOP) and the Sales and Operations Execution (SnOE) processes and associated plans. This position requires excellent collaboration skills, a rapport with executive leadership, and expertise in the FPGA supply chain as well as strong working knowledge of the FPGA market. Responsibilities include planning, managing, and executing technical and specialized supply chain programs or projects to meet specific objectives or goals. Understands the capability ecosystem and technical requirements of Intel's and Altera's partners and works closely with the internal development teams to guide the direction of the supply chain initiatives. Reviews and updates the project/program schedule(s) with all stakeholders that will be affected by the project/program activities and develops a comprehensive schedule while monitoring future work to proactively adjust for unforeseen scheduling challenges. Develops project/program indicators to manage program health including quality and timelines. Conducts project/program close out assessment, after action reviews and creates a recommendations report documenting the successful and unsuccessful project/program elements. Develops or supports the transition change management plan and communicates to all stakeholders via several communication methods. Drives and makes decisions within project/program boundary conditions and uses RAD model to drive effective decision making for the whole program.
In this role, your responsibilities will include:  
Drive the successful execution of the SnOP process and define clear decision ownership and responsibilities.
Responsible for gathering inputs, monitoring KPIs, and creating and maintaining the operating plan.
Coordinates with cross functional stakeholders to solve supply - demand gaps in the operating plan.
Serves as the subject matter expert for supply plan execution and SnOE governance.
Coordinates cross functional decision makers for inputs to exception management and resolution.
Responsible for stewarding the performance of the operating plan.
Accountable for conducting root cause analysis (RCAs) for identified deviations (actual vs plan) in SnOE and SnOP KPIs.
Leads meeting orchestration to ensure cross-functional alignment to present information to the executive leadership team for trade-off decisions.
Orchestrates resolution of escalations and / or executive approvals impacting priority products/customers.
Responsible for the performance of the SnOE and SnOP processes and implementing continuous improvement initiatives.
Qualifications:
You must possess the below minimum education requirements and minimum required qualifications to be initially considered for this position. Relevant experience can be obtained through schoolwork, classes, project work, internships, and/or military experience. Additional preferred qualifications are in addition to the minimum requirements and are considered a plus factor in identifying top candidates.
Minimum Qualifications:  
Bachelor’s Degree with 6 years of experience or Master’s Degree in related field with 4+ years of experience in supply chain, finance, economics, IE, or a related field.
4+ years of experience with handling business priorities and importance of business results, ensuring team attention is focused on most relevant topics.
4+ years of experience with supply chain processes and broad cross-functional knowledge across product, finance, sales, marketing, and procurement.
4+ years of experience managing SnOE and SnOP data, decision, people, and activities.
2+ years of experience orchestrating cross-functional team management and leadership.
This position is not eligible for Intel immigration sponsorship.
Preferred Qualifications:
Business acumen - understands ecosystem, business, and supply chain challenges and landscape.
Solid interpersonal skills - can bring difficult discussions to the table and arbitrate different viewpoints effectively, not afraid to speak up to ensure success of program and call out dysfunction.
Presence and Communications - can create and facilitate engagement and dialogue and open communication in stressful situations, can communicate goals and plans to leaders and critical project stakeholders.
Customer engagement skills and the ability to develop strong partnerships by gaining their trust and respect.
Job Type:
Experienced Hire
Shift:
Shift 1 (United States of America)
Primary Location:
US, California, San Jose
Additional Locations:
Business group:
The Programmable Solutions Group (PSG) was formed from the acquisition of Altera. As part of Intel, PSG will create market-leading programmable logic devices that deliver a wider range of capabilities than customers experience today. Combining Altera's industry-leading FPGA technology and customer support with Intel's world-class semiconductor manufacturing capabilities will enable customers to create the next generation of electronic systems with unmatched performance and power efficiency. PSG takes pride in creating an energetic and dynamic work environment that is driven by ingenuity and innovation. We believe the growth and success of our group is directly linked to the growth and satisfaction of our employees. That is why PSG is committed to a work environment that is flexible and collaborative, and allows our employees to reach their full potential.
Posting Statement:
All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, religious creed, sex, national origin, ancestry, age, physical or mental disability, medical condition, genetic information, military and veteran status, marital status, pregnancy, gender, gender expression, gender identity, sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by local law, regulation, or ordinance.
Position of Trust
N/A
Benefits:
We offer a total compensation package that ranks among the best in the industry. It consists of competitive pay, stock, bonuses, as well as, benefit programs which include health, retirement, and vacation. Find more information about all of our Amazing Benefits here: https://www.intel.com/content/www/us/en/jobs/benefits.html
Annual Salary Range for jobs which could be performed in the US:
$123,139.00-$203,801.00
Salary range dependent on a number of factors including location and experience.
Work Model for this Role
This role will be eligible for our hybrid work model which allows employees to split their time between working on-site at their assigned Intel site and off-site. * Job posting details (such as work model, location or time type) are subject to change.

item.title: Intel: DCAI Strategic Planning Sr Director
item.description:
locations
US, California, San Jose
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
job requisition id
JR0264644
Job Details:
Job Description:
Intel is seeking a dynamic and visionary leader to serve as the Senior Director – Strategic Planning and Roadmap for DCIA.
Responsibilities
Product Strategy and Roadmap:
Working closely with the VP Product strategy and planning, define the long-term vision and roadmap for the DCAI group at Intel.
This includes crafting the BoD presentations to articulate the strategy.
Assisting with the Long-range planning
Working closely with the market and competitive analysis team to identify opportunities and threats.
Working closely with executive stakeholders and business leaders to optimize resource allocation, align strategy and execution, and respond to changing market dynamics.
Translating business objectives into actionable product strategies
Driving transformation initiatives across the organization:
Working closely with cross-functional teams in strategy and, operations, finance, and systems and tools to execute on transformational initiatives (e.g., Cost optimizations, etc., process efficiencies, etc.)
Communication and Collaboration:
Communicate product strategy effectively to various stakeholders, including executives, and sales.
Metrics and Analysis:
Define key performance indicators (KPIs) to measure product success.
Analyze product data to identify areas for improvement and make data-driven decisions.
Track progress towards product goals and adjust the strategy as needed
Qualifications:
Minimum Qualifications
Bachelor’s degree in computer science, engineering, or business or related discipline with 12+ years of experience.
5+ years of experience across a multitude of executive roles and experiences.
5+ years of experience in recognizing external market shifts and technological trends, translating them into competitive opportunities, mobilizing the technical community to act.
Preferred Qualifications
Broad technical knowledge in Data Center and Artificial Intelligence.
Deep technical experience across cloud Si, software, networks, and systems.
Experience in management consulting,
Job Type:
Experienced Hire
Shift:
Shift 1 (United States of America)
Primary Location:
US, California, San Jose
Additional Locations:
Business group:
The Data Center & Artificial Intelligence Group (DCAI) is at the heart of Intel’s transformation from a PC company to a company that runs the cloud and billions of smart, connected computing devices. The data center is the underpinning for every data-driven service, from artificial intelligence to 5G to high-performance computing, and DCG delivers the products and technologies—spanning software, processors, storage, I/O, and networking solutions—that fuel cloud, communications, enterprise, and government data centers around the world.
Posting Statement:
All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, religious creed, sex, national origin, ancestry, age, physical or mental disability, medical condition, genetic information, military and veteran status, marital status, pregnancy, gender, gender expression, gender identity, sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by local law, regulation, or ordinance.
Position of Trust
This role is a Position of Trust. Should you accept this position, you must consent to and pass an extended Background Investigation, which includes (subject to country law), extended education, SEC sanctions, and additional criminal and civil checks. For internals, this investigation may or may not be completed prior to starting the position. For additional questions, please contact your Recruiter.
Benefits:
We offer a total compensation package that ranks among the best in the industry. It consists of competitive pay, stock, bonuses, as well as, benefit programs which include health, retirement, and vacation. Find more information about all of our Amazing Benefits here: https://www.intel.com/content/www/us/en/jobs/benefits.html
Annual Salary Range for jobs which could be performed in the US:
$195,317.00-$341,559.00
Salary range dependent on a number of factors including location and experience.
Work Model for this Role
This role will be eligible for our hybrid work model which allows employees to split their time between working on-site at their assigned Intel site and off-site. * Job posting details (such as work model, location or time type) are subject to change.

item.title: Intel: FPGA Soft IP Design Verification Lead Engineer
item.description:
locations
US, California, San Jose
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
job requisition id
JR0267358
Job Details:
Job Description:
In Q4 2023, Intel announced Altera will be reported as a separate business unit beginning on January 1, 2024, with ongoing support from Intel.
This position is associated to that standalone business strategy and is expected to fully transition to a standalone company at some time in the future.
Join Our Team: Altera, IPSE group is responsible for High-Speed Protocol IP portfolio. As Lead DV Engineer focusing on IP Verification and Validation, you will be responsible for carrying out design validation for Intel next generation IP's across the Intel FPGA IP product portfolios. The charter of IP verification and validation team is to verify and validate the IP for robust functionality from functional simulation and FPGA development kit based HW validation. The verification and validation areas encompass IP's for high-speed transceiver protocols.
Key Responsibilities:
· Create comprehensive verification and validation plan based on IP/FPGA architecture specifications and carry out all the IP validation tasks. The plan encompasses functional, system level and hardware verification and validation perspectives.
· Developing IP/subsystem/system level testbench, creating tests, and necessary coverage goals based on specification to verify the implementation. Writing directed and random test cases, debugging failures, filing and closing bugs.
· Reviewing verification and validation results against the coverage goals. Writing, analyzing and achieving coverage metrics.
· Work with cross-functional teams and prepare/support IP functional validation tests for IP bring-up on actual FPGA development kits maximizing FPGA hardware capability to bring substantial improvement to IP quality and usability for Altera FPGA IP product portfolios.
· Integration of Synopsys VIP and their usage to aid end to end testing Ethernet/MACSEC/IPSEC protocol testing.
· Developing verification and validation tools and flows, as needed.
· Apply advanced techniques to achieve verification and validation with the highest quality, productivity, and time-to-market.
· Excellent communication skills.
Qualifications:
You must possess the below minimum education requirements and minimum required qualifications to be initially considered for this position. Relevant experience can be obtained through schoolwork, classes, project work, internships, and/or military experience. Additional preferred qualifications are in addition to the minimum requirements and are considered a plus factor in identifying top candidates.
Minimum Requirements:
· BS/MS in Electrical Engineering, Computer Engineering or a closely related field of study plus 8 years of industry experience.
· 8+ years of experience developing verification collateral in Verilog, System Verilog, and UVM.
· 7 years with Ethernet protocol verification is required.
· Fluency in UVM, must have 7 years prior work experience with complex coverage driven random constraint UVM environments.
· Should have expertise in network security protocols: Ethernet/MACSEC/IPSEC end to end testing. Prefer candidates to be well versed in PCIe and Memory security solutions.
· 7 years of experience creating test plan from High level Specification and developing test cases.
· 7 years of experience of debugging skills to narrow down and isolate issue between RTL design and testbench or test case is required.
· 4+ years of leading complex projects and leadership experience working with cross functional teams.
Preferred Qualifications:
· Must have put together complex UVM environments from scratch.
· FPGA experience is a plus.
Job Type:
Experienced Hire
Shift:
Shift 1 (United States of America)
Primary Location:
US, California, San Jose
Additional Locations:
Business group:
The Programmable Solutions Group (PSG) was formed from the acquisition of Altera. As part of Intel, PSG will create market-leading programmable logic devices that deliver a wider range of capabilities than customers experience today. Combining Altera's industry-leading FPGA technology and customer support with Intel's world-class semiconductor manufacturing capabilities will enable customers to create the next generation of electronic systems with unmatched performance and power efficiency. PSG takes pride in creating an energetic and dynamic work environment that is driven by ingenuity and innovation. We believe the growth and success of our group is directly linked to the growth and satisfaction of our employees. That is why PSG is committed to a work environment that is flexible and collaborative, and allows our employees to reach their full potential.
Posting Statement:
All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, religious creed, sex, national origin, ancestry, age, physical or mental disability, medical condition, genetic information, military and veteran status, marital status, pregnancy, gender, gender expression, gender identity, sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by local law, regulation, or ordinance.
Position of Trust
N/A
Benefits:
We offer a total compensation package that ranks among the best in the industry. It consists of competitive pay, stock, bonuses, as well as, benefit programs which include health, retirement, and vacation. Find more information about all of our Amazing Benefits here: https://www.intel.com/content/www/us/en/jobs/benefits.html
Annual Salary Range for jobs which could be performed in the US:
$144,501.00-$217,311.00
Salary range dependent on a number of factors including location and experience.
Work Model for this Role
This role will be eligible for our hybrid work model which allows employees to split their time between working on-site at their assigned Intel site and off-site. * Job posting details (such as work model, location or time type) are subject to change.

item.title: Microchip: Principal Engineer-Device, SiC
item.description:
locations
AZ - Chandler
TX - Austin - Park Centre
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
job requisition id
R2431-24
Are you looking for a unique opportunity to be a part of something great? Want to join a 20,000-member team that works on the technology that powers the world around us? Looking for an atmosphere of trust, empowerment, respect, diversity, and communication? How about an opportunity to own a piece of a multi-billion dollar (with a B!) global organization? We offer all that and more at Microchip Technology, Inc.
People come to work at Microchip because we help design the technology that runs the world. They stay because our culture supports their growth and stability. They are challenged and driven by an incredible array of products and solutions with unlimited career potential. Microchip’s nationally-recognized Leadership Passage Programs support career growth where we proudly enroll over a thousand people annually. We take pride in our commitment to employee development, values-based decision making, and strong sense of community, driven by our Vision, Mission, and 11 Guiding Values; we affectionately refer to it as the Aggregate System and it’s won us countless awards for diversity and workplace excellence.
Our company is built by dedicated team players who love to challenge the status quo; we did not achieve record revenue and over 30 years of quarterly profitability without a great team dedicated to empowering innovation. People like you.
Visit our careers page to see what exciting opportunities and company perks await!
Job Description:
As a member in the Silicon Carbide (SiC) Design team, you will play a pivotal role in the design, development, and optimization of SiC power semiconductor devices. You will work closely with cross-functional teams, including process engineers, product engineers, test and design engineers, to ensure the successful implementation of innovative SiC solutions. This position requires a deep understanding of semiconductor device physics and design methodologies.
The successful candidate will be able to use their device physics and application knowledge to test, characterize and recommend improvements to high voltage SiC power device solutions.
Requirements/Qualifications:
Key Responsibilities:
Propose and review Silicon Carbide (SiC) semiconductor device concepts for MOSFETs, diodes, and other power devices.
Conduct simulations and modeling using Spice (or equivalents) to predict performance and identify areas for improvement.
Oversee detailed characterization and analysis of power devices to validate design and process changes.
Creating custom test bench setups and test fixtures as needed for high voltage devices.
Work with engineering teams to ensure long-term performance and reliability of SiC devices.
Provide technical guidance and mentorship to junior engineers and team members.
Prepare and present technical reports, documentation, and presentations to internal and external stakeholders.
Ability to review and troubleshoot power application boards and modules.
Qualifications:
Ph.D. or Master’s degree in Semiconductor Device Physics, Electrical Engineering or a related field.
Minimum of 6.5 years of experience in semiconductors, with a focus on device performance within typical applications
Strong understanding of semiconductor device physics and SiC material properties.
Proficiency in device simulation tools (e.g., Spice, MATLAB, etc.) and modeling techniques.
Excellent problem-solving skills and the ability to work effectively in a collaborative team environment.
Strong communication skills, both written and verbal, with the ability to convey complex technical concepts to diverse audiences.
Preferred Qualifications:
Experience with semiconductor physics for high voltage power devices and their applications
Familiar with semiconductor fabrication processes and characterization techniques.
Familiarity with industry standards and best practices
Travel Time:
0% - 25%
Physical Attributes:
Crouching, Feeling, Handling, Hearing, Kneeling, Other, Pulling, Pushing, Reaching, Seeing, Talking, Works Alone, Works Around Others
Physical Requirements:
80% Sitting, 10% Standing, 10% Walking
Microchip Technology Inc is an equal opportunity/affirmative action employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, color, religion, national origin, disability, protected Veteran status, age, or any other characteristic protected by law.

For more information on applicable equal employment regulations, please refer to the EEO is the Law Poster and the EEO is the Law Poster Supplement. Please also refer to the Pay Transparency Policy Statement.

item.title: Microchip: Senior Software Engineer
item.description:
locations
Canada - Montreal
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
job requisition id
R2319-24
Are you looking for a unique opportunity to be a part of something great? Want to join a 20,000-member team that works on the technology that powers the world around us? Looking for an atmosphere of trust, empowerment, respect, diversity, and communication? How about an opportunity to own a piece of a multi-billion dollar (with a B!) global organization? We offer all that and more at Microchip Technology, Inc.
People come to work at Microchip because we help design the technology that runs the world. They stay because our culture supports their growth and stability. They are challenged and driven by an incredible array of products and solutions with unlimited career potential. Microchip’s nationally-recognized Leadership Passage Programs support career growth where we proudly enroll over a thousand people annually. We take pride in our commitment to employee development, values-based decision making, and strong sense of community, driven by our Vision, Mission, and 11 Guiding Values; we affectionately refer to it as the Aggregate System and it’s won us countless awards for diversity and workplace excellence.
Our company is built by dedicated team players who love to challenge the status quo; we did not achieve record revenue and over 30 years of quarterly profitability without a great team dedicated to empowering innovation. People like you.
Visit our careers page to see what exciting opportunities and company perks await!
Job Description:
Participate in the development of Linux support for a high performance multi-core RISC-V processor
Develop Linux device drivers for Ethernet, PCIe and other interfaces.
Development of Linux application libraries, including performance benchmarking and optimization.
Collaborate with other team members in functional and system level testing.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Participer au développement du support Linux pour un processeur RISC-V multi-cœur haute performance.
Développer des pilotes de périphériques Linux pour Ethernet, PCIe et autres interfaces.
Développer des bibliothèques d'applications Linux, y compris le benchmarking de performance et l'optimisation.
Collaborer avec les autres membres de l'équipe pour les tests fonctionnels et au niveau système.
Requirements/Qualifications:
Bachelor’s or Master’s Degree in Computer/Electrical Engineering, Computer Science or equivalent
5+ years of related work experience in Software development for embedded systems.
Expert C knowledge with Python as an asset.
Solid experience with Linux development, including drivers, kernel configuration and Buildroot/Yocto environments.
Experience with RTOS environments is also an asset.
Experience developing high quality code from concept through to production.
Excellent analytical, communication, and documentation skills.
Strong interpersonal skills, experience as part of a collaborative development team.
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Diplôme de Bachelor ou Master en Informatique/Ingénierie Électrique, Informatique ou équivalent.
Plus de 5 ans d'expérience professionnelle dans le développement de logiciels pour systèmes embarqués.
Expertise en C avec des connaissances en Python comme atout.
Solide expérience en développement sous Linux, y compris les pilotes, la configuration du noyau et les environnements Buildroot/Yocto.
L'expérience avec les environnements RTOS est également un atout.
Expérience dans le développement de code de haute qualité, de la conception à la production.
Excellentes compétences analytiques, de communication et de documentation.
Forte aptitude relationnelle, expérience en tant que membre d'une équipe de développement collaborative.
Travel Time:
0% - 25%
